facts.text: Simone Biles has the most World Championship gold medals of any gymnast in history, male or female.
The balance beam is only 10 centimeters (about 4 inches) wide, roughly the width of a smartphone.
Nadia Comaneci scored the first perfect 10 in Olympic gymnastics history at the 1976 Montreal Games.
The Yurchenko vault, first performed by Natalia Yurchenko in 1982, involves a round-off entry onto the springboard followed by a back handspring onto the vault table.
Rhythmic gymnastics became an official Olympic sport at the 1984 Los Angeles Games.
The pommel horse is considered one of the most technically difficult apparatus in men's gymnastics.
UCLA's gymnastics program has won the most NCAA women's gymnastics team titles.
Ancient Greeks practiced gymnastics as a form of training for warfare, and the word 'gymnastics' comes from the Greek word 'gymnazein,' meaning to exercise naked.
The Fédération Internationale de Gymnastique (FIG) was founded in 1881, making it one of the oldest international sports federations.
Simone Biles has four gymnastics skills named after her across different apparatus.
Kohei Uchimura of Japan won six consecutive all-around World Championship titles from 2009 to 2015.
The open-ended Code of Points scoring system replaced the traditional perfect 10 system after the 2004 Athens Olympics.
Kerri Strug famously landed her second vault on an injured ankle to clinch team gold for the USA at the 1996 Atlanta Olympics.
In rhythmic gymnastics, athletes perform with five apparatus: ribbon, ball, hoop, clubs, and rope.
Trampoline gymnastics was added to the Olympic program at the 2000 Sydney Games.
The Tkachev release move on uneven bars is named after Soviet gymnast Alexander Tkachev, who first performed it in 1977.
Elite gymnasts typically train 30 to 40 hours per week.
Olga Korbut's daring back flip on the uneven bars at the 1972 Munich Olympics captivated the world and sparked a gymnastics boom.
The men's artistic gymnastics program includes six apparatus: floor exercise, pommel horse, rings, vault, parallel bars, and horizontal bar.
Gabby Douglas became the first African American woman to win the individual all-around gold medal at the 2012 London Olympics.
The difficulty score (D-score) in modern gymnastics has no upper limit, encouraging gymnasts to attempt increasingly complex skills.
Mary Lou Retton became the first American woman to win the Olympic all-around gold medal at the 1984 Los Angeles Games.
Rhythmic gymnasts must display incredible flexibility, with many able to achieve over 180-degree splits.
The horizontal bar (high bar) is the apparatus where gymnasts perform some of the most dramatic release moves and giant swings.
Oklahoma has been a dominant force in NCAA women's gymnastics, winning multiple national titles.
Gymnastics was included in the very first modern Olympic Games in Athens in 1896.
Simone Biles withdrew from several events at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics citing the 'twisties,' bringing global attention to mental health in sports.
The rings apparatus in men's gymnastics requires extraordinary upper body strength, with athletes holding static positions for several seconds.

A gymnast's execution score (E-score) starts at 10.0 and deductions are taken for errors in form, technique, and landing.

The parallel bars event requires athletes to perform swings, holds, and aerial maneuvers between two bars set at the same height.

Larisa Latynina of the Soviet Union held the record for most Olympic medals by any athlete (18) for 48 years until Michael Phelps surpassed her in 2012.
The vault event takes just a few seconds but requires an explosive combination of speed, power, and aerial awareness.

Floor exercise routines in women's gymnastics are performed to music, while men's floor routines are not.
The FIG Code of Points is updated every four years, typically after each Olympic cycle.

Chinese gymnasts have historically dominated the rings and parallel bars events at the Olympics.
The Biles on floor exercise is a double layout with a half twist, a skill so difficult no other woman has competed it.
Gymnastics scoring underwent a major overhaul in 2006 after judging controversies at the 2004 Athens Olympics.

The uneven bars are set approximately 180 cm apart and at different heights, requiring dynamic transitions between the two.

The US women's gymnastics team was called the 'Final Five' because Marta Karolyi was retiring as coordinator.
A Tkachev on uneven bars can be performed in straddle, piked, or layout position, each with increasing difficulty.
Dong Dong of China won trampoline medals at three consecutive Olympics (2008, 2012, 2016).
The compulsory routine system was eliminated from Olympic gymnastics after the 1996 Atlanta Games.
A front tuck is one of the first somersault skills gymnasts learn in their development.
Eythora Thorsdottir of the Netherlands was known for her highly artistic and creative floor exercise routines.
The vault difficulty score typically ranges from about 4.0 for basic vaults to over 7.0 for the hardest vaults.
Mykayla Skinner's comeback to gymnastics after retiring demonstrated the sport's evolving age dynamics.
In trampoline gymnastics, athletes are judged on execution, difficulty, time of flight, and horizontal displacement.
Elisabeth Seitz of Germany competed in gymnastics at four consecutive Olympics.
The Garrison back somersault on balance beam was named after US gymnast Lori Garrison.
A toe shoot on uneven bars uses the feet pressing on the low bar to propel the gymnast to the high bar.
Simone Biles has been named AP Female Athlete of the Year multiple times.
The FIG Congress meets every two years to vote on rule changes and elect leadership.
UCLA gymnast Kyla Ross scored 33 career perfect 10s during her NCAA career.
A double turn on balance beam requires the gymnast to complete two full rotations on one foot.
Gymnastics events are broadcast in over 100 countries during the Olympic Games.
The European Gymnastics Championships are the continent's premier gymnastics competition outside of the Olympics and Worlds.
A Y-turn on balance beam involves lifting one leg to a vertical position while turning.
Brody Malone became US men's all-around champion in 2021 and 2022.
Rhythmic gymnasts typically retire earlier than artistic gymnasts, often in their early twenties.
The first gymnastics club in the United States was formed in 1848.
An L-sit on rings or parallel bars requires holding the body in a seated position with legs extended horizontally.
Carlos Yulo of the Philippines became the first male Filipino Olympic gold medalist in any sport when he won floor exercise gold in 2024.
The Pan American Games include artistic, rhythmic, and trampoline gymnastics events.
A Diamidov on parallel bars is a full pirouette turn in support position.
Gymnastics can improve bone density, which helps prevent osteoporosis later in life.
The Goodwill Games in 1986 featured top-level gymnastics competition between Cold War rivals.
A back layout is a backward somersault with the body fully extended.
Stephen Nedoroscik, a pommel horse specialist, became a viral sensation at the 2024 Paris Olympics.
The American Cup is one of the most prestigious international gymnastics invitationals, held annually in the United States.
Wrist supports called 'tiger paws' are commonly used by young gymnasts to prevent wrist pain during tumbling.
